title: Glebionis roxburghii

This plant belongs to the genus Glebionis, which is a group of flowering plants that are often characterized by their daisy-like appearance. The classification of Glebionis roxburghii reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history.

intro: Glebionis roxburghii, also known as Roxburgh's daisy, is an annual herbaceous plant native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help in treating various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
